authoritative_sources.title: DCMA Manual 2303-01, Volume 1, Surveillance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCMA is the DoD agency responsible for contract administration, and this public manual defines surveillance planning, execution, documentation, PDREP use, risk assessment, and escalation posture for defense contractor performance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Defense execution surveillance, evidence capture, and escalation framing

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 46 - Quality Assurance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR Part 46 is the federal acquisition baseline for government contract quality assurance, inspection, acceptance, and nonconforming supplies, making it authoritative for defense operations release and acceptance boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Contract quality, source inspection, acceptance, and nonconformance boundary handling
used_for: separation between operations status and formal quality acceptance

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7007 - Contractor Counterfeit Electronic Part Detection and Avoidance System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DFARS clause is a defense-specific contract quality source for counterfeit electronic part detection, suspect-part handling, risk-based inspection, supplier flowdown, and source-control escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Counterfeit-risk hold, supplier source evidence, and release-blocker handling
used_for: quality hold and suspect-part evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7008 - Sources of Electronic Parts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DFARS source-of-electronic-parts clause defines defense source hierarchy and documentation expectations, making it authoritative for supplier substitution and unapproved-source refusal scenarios.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.211-7003 - Item Unique Identification and Valuation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DFARS IUID clause is authoritative for defense item unique identification, serial, lot, batch, machine-readable marking, and reporting expectations used to prove item identity and delivery traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IUID, serial, lot, delivery, and traceability evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: DoD Manual 4140.01, Volume 5 - DoD Supply Chain Materiel Management Procedures: Delivery of Materiel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDM 4140.01 Volume 5 is official DoD supply-chain procedure for delivery of materiel, receipt, issue, in-transit, inventory, and distribution control evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Materiel delivery, inventory movement, receipt, issue, and readiness confirmation
used_for: mission support readiness and handoff dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: DoD Instruction 5000.64 - Accountability and Management of DoD Equipment and Other Accountable Property
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDI 5000.64 is official DoD policy for accountable property records, APSR use, lifecycle events, transfers, physical inventories, IUID-enabled accountability, and property-loss handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accountable property, custody, transfer, and completion evidence handling
used_for: property discrepancy and completion refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: 15 CFR Part 700 - Defense Priorities and Allocations System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 15 CFR Part 700 is the public DPAS regulation for rated orders, acceptance, rejection, preferential scheduling, and flowdown, making it authoritative for defense priority conflicts and mission-critical throughput boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Rated-order priority, preferential scheduling, and priority-conflict escalation
used_for: human escalation when priority conflicts require authority

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized steward of the 9100-series quality management standard for aviation, space, and defense organizations, making it authoritative for defense QMS vocabulary and control posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Aerospace-and-defense quality management and evidence posture
used_for: separating operations handling from final quality authority

workflow_stages.name: intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, program, contract, CLIN or work package, lifecycle state, systems of record, tenant authority rules, source-control constraints, and mission or rated-order context before any execution-state change is recommended.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: materiel, traceability, property, and readiness confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile lot, batch, serial, IUID, supplier, inventory, APSR, WAWF or receiving-report, and logistics evidence across ERP, MES, WMS, supplier, property, and logistics systems before work advances.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: in-process execution and lifecycle-state progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance, hold, or rework accepted defense work based on documented execution state, shortage posture, source-inspection status, property custody, operator handoff evidence, nonconformance signals, and mission-impact blockers without absorbing approval authority.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, supplier, quality, property, and release-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop routine execution when traceability conflicts, quality holds, counterfeit-risk signals, source-control gaps, supplier substitution pressure, property-accountability discrepancies, DPAS priority conflicts, or formal acceptance boundaries change whether work may continue.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and safe closure
workflow_stages.description: Document completion only when the closure checklist, scan or IUID evidence, receiving-report or handoff evidence, inventory and property state, traceability records, and release-dependent artifacts are explicit and auditable.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, work packages, travelers, routers, operator instructions, completion checklists, and mission-support task records
contract, CLIN or SLIN, CDRL, program, customer, rated-order, and priority context
batch or lot records, serial numbers, IUID or UID marks, UID data matrix scans, IUID Registry or WAWF references, and traceability logs
inventory states, materiel release or issue records, kitting records, storage-condition records, warehouse picks, in-transit records, and shortage logs
supplier records, approved-source constraints, certificates of conformance, ASNs, source-inspection artifacts, substitution requests, and counterfeit-risk records
quality holds, nonconformance reports, containment records, source inspection results, release records, rework authorizations, deviation or waiver records, and change-control documentation
government-furnished property records, APSR records, custody receipts, transfer records, physical inventory evidence, property discrepancy or loss records, and closeout artifacts
handoff notes, mission-impact notes, receiving reports, DD250 or WAWF evidence, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: IUID Registry or tenant UID repository

allowed: classify accepted defense operations work by lifecycle state, blocker posture, and next action
advance, hold, return for rework, or document completion when the controlling production, materiel, traceability, property, supplier, quality, release, and handoff evidence is complete and consistent
prepare operational readiness summaries, mission-impact blocker notes, exception summaries, completion records, and escalation packets tied to named evidence
normalize defense terminology into authoritative records before making a bounded execution recommendation
escalate: quality hold release, source inspection acceptance, QAR action, or nonconformance disposition
engineering deviation, configuration change, airworthiness, safety, hazardous-material, weapons-system, or mission-release authority
contracting officer, administrative contracting officer, contract modification, rated-order rejection, or DPAS priority conflict authority
supplier approval, source approval, counterfeit-risk disposition, or substitution approval
property administrator authority, property-loss investigation, physical inventory certification, or financial liability action
classified-program handling, export-control interpretation, legal interpretation, or security officer authority
refuse: mark work complete without completion, receiving-report, handoff, IUID, property, traceability, or release evidence
move, issue, ship, or close held or unreleased product because of schedule or mission pressure
approve source changes, supplier substitutions, counterfeit-risk escapes, or source-control exceptions
treat missing or conflicting lot, serial, IUID, APSR, WAWF, supplier, inventory, or quality records as acceptable proof
impersonate engineering, contracting, quality, government acceptance, property, security, safety, legal, or executive authority

domain_failure_modes: traceability records conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, APSR, WAWF, supplier, and IUID evidence
product or equipment is pressured toward movement, issue, shipment, or closure while quality hold, source inspection, NCR, release, property, or receiving-report evidence is incomplete
counterfeit electronic part, unapproved source, or supplier substitution risk is treated as routine operations work
mission or rated-order pressure is used to bypass quality, source, traceability, property, or release controls
government-furnished property custody, APSR status, or transfer evidence is missing but work is marked complete
operations status drifts into engineering, contracting, QAR, source approval, security, safety, or government acceptance authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Contract-specific clauses and classified program instructions
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve the governing contract, CDRL, security guide, program quality plan, release matrix, and tenant authority map before execution recommendations that depend on them.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Actual tenant systems of record and record locations
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve tenant ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, APSR, WAWF, IUID, PDREP, and document-control record links at runtime before acting.

notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because uncertain contract, classified, authority, and tenant-system facts are converted into runtime prerequisites and refusal triggers.

The public baseline constrains defense operations evidence handling but does not create engineering, contracting, quality, property, security, safety, supplier approval, or government acceptance authority.
